items.front: How do helicopter/powered-lift taxi operations differ from fixed-wing?
items.back: Helicopters and powered-lift may air taxi, hover taxi, or surface taxi — each with a different relationship to the surface.

items.front: From where may a helicopter takeoff clearance be issued?
items.back: From locations other than the runway, as long as the location is suitable.

items.front: Why do helicopter departures have different separation standards?
items.back: Helicopters have unique wake turbulence characteristics and can operate with different separation than fixed-wing aircraft.

items.front: Why do helicopter arrivals have different separation standards?
items.back: Helicopters can operate at lower speeds and with steeper approaches, allowing different separation minima.

items.front: What is the key condition for simultaneous helicopter operations?
items.back: The operations must not adversely affect other aircraft.

items.front: What must be ensured when issuing a helicopter landing clearance to a non-runway location?
items.back: The landing area must be suitable and the operation must not conflict with other traffic on the movement area.
